resposta:
solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def compress(self, chars: List[str]) -> int:
        i = 0
        count = 1
        for j in range(1, len(chars) + 1):
            if j == len(chars) or chars[j] != chars[j - 1]:
                chars[i] = chars[j - 1]
                i += 1
                if count > 1:
                    for k in str(count):
                        chars[i] = k
                        i += 1
                count = 1
            else:
                count += 1
        return i
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses a two-pointer approach to iterate through the input array. It keeps track of the current character and the count of consecutive repeating characters. When it encounters a new character or reaches the end of the array, it appends the current character and the count to the result array. The solution has a high efficiency due to its constant extra space usage and linear time complexity.
